_calibration: CALIBRATION FIXTURE (known-bad, deliberately committed): the rule-polarity class (caution.md C-046/C-047/C-048), Compliance Object Model shape. website_obligations[0].duty carries clear prohibition language ('is an offence to advertise') but is typed evidence_type='presence' - inverted, so the linter's naive presence-style check would fire a breach when the prohibited content is ABSENT and stay silent when it is PRESENT, exactly the DG-02 defect (an ASA must_appear rule firing on absence). catalogue/linters/polarity.js MUST flag this as polarity-prohibition-mismatch in --calibrate mode, or it has not earned its zero.

records.name: Prescription-only medicine advertising restriction (calibration fixture: inverted polarity)
excluded_when: no advertising of prescription-only medicines to the public

website_obligations.duty: It is an offence to advertise prescription-only medicines directly to members of the public
elements: no prescription-only medicine names or claims in public-facing advertising

regulator.name: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA)
